title: NOC 10012 – Purchasing Managers | GTR Immigration
description: Purchasing Managers classified under NOC 10012 fall within one of the most senior and in-demand occupational groups in Canada’s immigration system. These professionals play a critical role in procurement strategy, supplier management, and cost optimization—making them attractive candidates for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).
duties: Plan, organize, direct, control, and evaluate purchasing activities
Develop procurement policies, procedures, and departmental budgets
Identify and evaluate suppliers for materials, equipment, and services
Assess cost, quality, and delivery performance
Negotiate or oversee major purchase contracts
Contribute to equipment and product specifications
Recruit, train, and supervise purchasing staff
employment_requirements: Occupations in TEER 0 usually require a university degree or college diploma and several years of experience in a related field.
job_outlook: Over the period 2024–2033, Canada is expected to create 8,800 new positions for Purchasing Managers, while approximately 9,000 job seekers may enter the occupation. For internationally trained professionals—particularly applicants from India—this presents encouraging opportunities when supported by a strategic immigration plan.
labour_market_demand: Purchasing Managers are in high demand across Canada’s major sectors, including finance, operations, manufacturing, logistics, and supply-chain management. Demand under this TEER category expanded rapidly following the pandemic and is projected to remain strong over the coming years.
Strong hiring prospects across provinces and territories
Excellent immigration prospects for qualified overseas professionals
Annual earnings commonly range from $57,000 to $160,346, depending on experience, location, and specialization.
Professionals immigrating under NOC 10012 benefit from:
Strong labour protections and work-life balance
Clear pathways to Canadian Permanent Residence (PR)

NOC 10012 refers to professionals responsible for planning, directing, and controlling purchasing and procurement operations within an organization. Under Canada’s National Occupational Classification system, this role is categorized at TEER 0, highlighting its senior-management status for immigration planning.
These professionals oversee vendor selection, contract negotiation, procurement policies, and supply-chain efficiency—ensuring organizations acquire goods and services at optimal cost and quality.
benefits: In addition to salary, many employers provide:
Employer contributions to national pension and employment-insurance programs
additional_information: 10 – Specialized middle management occupations in administrative services, financial and business services, and communication (except broadcasting)
100 – Specialized middle management occupations in administrative services, financial and business services, and communication (except broadcasting)
